title: CSAR Configuration
description: Configuration schema for CSAR (Coordinated Stateless API Router). Validates config.yaml files with autocomplete support.

include.description: List of file paths or glob patterns to merge into this config. Paths are resolved relative to the directory of the file containing the include.

profile.description: Deployment profile. Enforces security constraints at startup.

listen_addr.description: HTTP(S) listen address for the API gateway.

security_profiles.description: Named reusable security configurations. Routes reference them via x-csar-security: "profile_name".

circuit_breakers.description: Named circuit breaker profiles referenced by x-csar-resilience.circuit_breaker.

throttling_policies.description: Named reusable throttling configurations. Routes reference them via x-csar-traffic: "policy_name" or x-csar-traffic.use.

cors_policies.description: Named reusable CORS configurations. Routes reference them via x-csar-cors: "policy_name".

cache_policies.description: Named reusable response cache configurations. Routes reference them via x-csar-cache: "policy_name" or x-csar-cache.use.

cache_invalidation_policies.description: Named reusable cache invalidation configurations. Routes reference them via x-csar-cache-invalidate: "policy_name" or x-csar-cache-invalidate.use.

retry_policies.description: Named reusable retry configurations. Routes reference them via x-csar-retry: "policy_name".

redact_policies.description: Named reusable redaction configurations. Routes reference them via x-csar-redact: "policy_name".

auth_validate_policies.description: Named reusable auth validation configurations. Routes reference them via x-csar-authn-validate: "policy_name".

authz_policies.description: Named reusable authorization configurations. Routes reference them via x-csar-authz: "policy_name" or x-csar-authz.use: "policy_name".

backend_tls_policies.description: Named reusable outbound TLS configurations. Routes reference them via x-csar-backend.tls: "policy_name" or x-csar-backend.tls.use: "policy_name". Policy definitions are terminal — they do not support the "use" field.

backend_pools.description: Named outbound HTTP transport pools. Routes reference them via x-csar-backend.pool to isolate upstream failure domains.

paths.description: Route definitions using OpenAPI-style x-csar-* extensions. Keys are URL path patterns.

TLSConfig.description: Inbound TLS configuration (clients connecting to CSAR).

key_file.description: Path to the TLS private key (PEM).

client_ca_file.description: Path to the CA that signed client certs. Enables mutual TLS.

CoordinatorConfig.description: Settings for the gRPC control plane connection.

ca_file.description: Path to the CA certificate for verifying the coordinator server.

cert_file.description: Client certificate (PEM) for mTLS to the coordinator.

key_file.description: Client private key (PEM) for mTLS to the coordinator.

allow_insecure.description: Permit plaintext gRPC to the coordinator. Development only.

invalidation_buffer_size.description: Number of token invalidation events to buffer for replay on router reconnect.

AccessControlConfig.description: IP-based access control. Supports individual IPs and CIDR ranges (IPv4/IPv6).

allow_cidrs.description: Allowed client IP addresses or CIDR ranges. Only matching clients are permitted.

trust_proxy.description: Trust X-Forwarded-For / X-Real-IP headers for extracting the real client IP.

SecurityPolicyConfig.description: Environment-level security constraints. In "prod" mode, insecure options are rejected at startup.

forbid_insecure_in_prod.description: Reject configurations using allow_insecure or omitting TLS in production.

require_mtls_for_coordinator.description: Require mTLS between routers and the coordinator in production.

redact_sensitive_logs.description: Redact token values, KMS key IDs, and security-relevant fields from structured logs.

SSRFConfig.description: SSRF protection for outbound proxy connections. All options default to true (block).

block_private.description: Block connections to RFC 1918 private subnets (10.x, 172.16.x, 192.168.x).

block_link_local.description: Block connections to link-local addresses (169.254.0.0/16, fe80::/10).

block_metadata.description: Block connections to cloud metadata endpoints (169.254.169.254).

allowed_internal_hosts.description: Explicit allowlist of internal hosts that may be accessed despite SSRF protections.

KMSConfig.description: Global Key Management Service configuration for token encryption/decryption.

default_key_id.description: Default KMS key used when a route doesn't specify one.

local_keys.description: Key=passphrase mappings for the "local" provider. For development only.

jitter.description: Add random jitter to retry delays to prevent thundering herd.

KMSCacheConfig.description: In-memory caching for KMS decrypt results.

max_entries.description: Maximum number of cached entries (prevents memory exhaustion).

auth_mode.description: Credential source for Yandex Cloud authentication.

oauth_token.description: Personal OAuth token — auto-refreshed to IAM token.

sa_key_file.description: Path to service account JSON key file. Recommended for production outside Yandex Cloud VMs.

RedisConfig.description: Redis connection for distributed rate limiting. Required when any route uses traffic.backend: "redis".

key_prefix.description: Prefix for all rate limiting keys.

max_requests.description: Maximum number of requests allowed in half-open state.

interval.description: Cyclic period of the closed state for clearing internal counts.

timeout.description: Duration of the open state before transitioning to half-open.

failure_threshold.description: Number of consecutive failures before opening the circuit.

max_wait.description: Maximum time a request can wait in the queue.

key.description: Dynamic throttle key template for per-entity rate limiting. Supports {query.param} and {header.Name} placeholders.

exclude_ips.description: IPs/CIDRs that bypass this throttle entirely.

vip_overrides.description: Header-based throttle policy switching for VIP clients.

client_limit_mode.description: How to handle X-CSAR-Client-Limit header from SDK clients.

GlobalThrottleConfig.description: Global rate limit safety net applied to ALL routes before per-route throttle. Uses fast in-memory atomic counter.

rate.description: Global requests per second limit across all routes.

max_wait.description: Maximum time a request can wait. "0s" = reject immediately when exceeded.

VIPOverride.description: Maps header values to alternate throttling policies.

values.description: Maps header values to throttling policy names.

SecurityConfig.description: Token injection via KMS. Fetches encrypted tokens, decrypts via KMS, injects into upstream headers.

profile.description: Reference to a named security_profiles entry. Inline fields override the profile's values.

token_ref.description: Reference passed to AuthService to fetch the encrypted token. Supports {query.param} and {header.Name} placeholders.

token_version.description: Opaque version string for cache invalidation. Bump when rotating tokens.

inject_header.description: HTTP header to inject the decrypted token into.

inject_format.description: Format template for the header value. Use {token} as placeholder.

on_kms_error.description: Behavior when the KMS provider is unavailable.

strip_token_params.description: Remove query parameters used in token_ref placeholders before forwarding to upstream.

SecurityConfigs.description: Security configuration. Supports a profile name (string), a single inline object, or an array of mixed strings and objects.

oneOf.description: Array of security configs (profile refs and/or inline objects).

TrafficConfig.description: Rate limiting / traffic shaping. Supports a policy name (string) or an inline object.

use.description: Reference to a throttling_policies entry. Inline fields override the policy's values.

max_wait.description: Maximum time a request can wait in the queue.

key.description: Dynamic throttle key template for per-entity rate limiting.

exclude_ips.description: IPs/CIDRs that bypass this throttle entirely.

vip_overrides.description: Header-based throttle policy switching for VIP clients.

client_limit_mode.description: How to handle X-CSAR-Client-Limit header from SDK clients.

AdaptiveBackpressureConfig.description: Upstream backpressure awareness. Reads Retry-After / X-RateLimit-Reset from upstream 429 responses and suspends the token bucket.

respect_headers.description: Ordered list of response headers to check for wait time.

suspend_bucket.description: Pause token generation for the route when backpressure is detected.

max_body_buffer.description: Maximum request body size (bytes) buffered for transparent retry replay. Default: 10 MiB.

RetryConfig.description: Retry configuration. Supports a policy name (string) or an inline object.

RetryConfigObject.description: Automatic retry with exponential backoff for upstream requests.

use.description: Reference to a retry_policies entry. Inline fields override the policy's values.

backoff.description: Base delay between retries (exponential backoff with jitter).

retryable_status_codes.description: HTTP status codes that trigger a retry.

auto_retry_429.description: Transparently retry upstream 429 responses using Retry-After delay. Client sees 200 instead of 429.

max_internal_wait.description: Maximum time to hold client connection while waiting for upstream backpressure to pass.

CORSConfig.description: CORS configuration. Supports a policy name (string) or an inline object.

CORSConfigObject.description: Cross-Origin Resource Sharing configuration with automatic preflight handling.

use.description: Reference to a cors_policies entry. Inline fields override the policy's values.

allowed_origins.description: Allowed origins. Use "*" to allow all (not recommended for production).

allow_credentials.description: Whether the request can include cookies.

max_age.description: Max time (seconds) a preflight response can be cached.

RedactConfig.description: Response payload redaction (DLP). Supports a policy name (string) or an inline object.

RedactConfigObject.description: Masks specified JSON fields in upstream responses before returning to clients.

use.description: Reference to a redact_policies entry. Inline fields override the policy's values.

fields.description: JSON field paths to redact. Supports dot notation and wildcards.

enabled.description: Toggle redaction without removing the config.

AuthValidateConfig.description: Inbound JWT/JWKS validation. Supports a policy name (string) or an inline object.

AuthValidateConfigObject.description: Validates inbound bearer tokens against a JWKS endpoint before proxying.

use.description: Reference to an auth_validate_policies entry. Inline fields override the policy's values.

jwks_url.description: Endpoint serving the JSON Web Key Set.

jwks_tls.description: Reference to a backend_tls_policies entry for the JWKS fetch client.

issuer.description: Validates the "iss" claim matches this value.

audiences.description: Validates the "aud" claim contains at least one of these entries.

token_prefix.description: Prefix stripped from the header value before parsing.

cache_ttl.description: How long JWKS keys are cached.

required_claims.description: Claim key=value pairs that must be present in the token.

forward_claims.description: Copy JWT claims into request headers. Map key = claim name, value = header name.

cookie_name.description: Read the JWT from a named cookie instead of a request header. When set, header_name and token_prefix are ignored.

CacheConfig.description: HTTP response caching. Supports a policy name (string) or an inline object.

CacheConfigObject.description: HTTP response caching for idempotent requests. Supports memory or Redis stores, dynamic keys, TTL rules, tags, and fail-open behavior.

use.description: Reference to a cache_policies entry. Scalar inline fields override policy values; list fields are additive.

enabled.description: Toggle caching without removing the config.

fail_mode.description: Failure behavior when the cache store is unavailable.

operation_timeout.description: Maximum time spent on each cache store operation.

ttl.description: Default cache TTL if no Cache-Control header is present.

ttl_jitter.description: Maximum bounded TTL reduction as a duration or percentage. Jitter never extends TTL.

key_query.description: Normalized query-string component included in the cache key.

stale_if_error.description: How long expired entries may be served when an upstream refresh fails.

stale_while_revalidate.description: How long expired entries may be served while one refresh is in progress.

content_types.description: Response Content-Type values eligible for cache writes.

response_ttl_rules.description: Ordered conditional TTL overrides based on trusted upstream response headers.

response_tags.description: Cache tags derived from trusted upstream response headers with CSAR-controlled prefixes.

namespaces.description: Namespace templates whose current versions are included in cache keys.

coalesce.description: Request coalescing settings for cache misses.

tags.description: Dynamic cache invalidation tags attached to stored responses.

vary_headers.description: Explicit request headers included in the cache key.

max_entries.description: Maximum number of cached responses (LRU eviction).

max_body_size.description: Maximum response body size to cache (bytes). Larger responses are not cached.

cache_statuses.description: Upstream response statuses eligible for caching. Supports exact statuses like "200" and classes like "2xx".

CacheTTLRule.description: Conditional TTL override for response caching.

from.description: Query parameter containing the start date.

to.description: Query parameter containing the end date.

ttl.description: TTL applied when the rule matches.

include.description: Query parameters included in the normalized key component. Empty means include all except excluded names.

exclude.description: Query parameters ignored when building the normalized key component.

sort.description: Sort query parameter names and repeated values before encoding.

drop_empty.description: Drop empty query parameter values from the normalized component.

CacheResponseTTLRule.description: Conditional TTL override based on trusted upstream response metadata.

ttl.description: TTL applied when the rule matches.

CacheResponseTag.description: Cache tag derivation from a trusted upstream response header.

header.description: Upstream response header containing comma-separated tag suffixes or values.

prefix.description: CSAR-controlled tag prefix template. Supports cache placeholders such as {tenant}, {subject}, {path.name}, and {query.name}.

CacheBypassHeader.description: One authorized cache bypass request header.

value.description: Optional exact header value required for bypass.

require_gateway_scope.description: Trusted gateway scope required before this bypass is honored.

wait.description: Maximum follower wait for the leader response.

wait_timeout_status.description: HTTP status returned to followers when the wait expires.

CacheInvalidationConfig.description: Tag-based response cache invalidation. Supports a policy name (string) or an inline object.

CacheInvalidationConfigObject.description: Tag-based response cache invalidation after successful upstream mutation responses.

use.description: Reference to a cache_invalidation_policies entry. Inline tags and status patterns are additive.

enabled.description: Toggle invalidation without removing the config.

store.description: Cache store to invalidate. Omit to invalidate all configured stores.

operation_timeout.description: Maximum time spent on each invalidation operation.

bump_namespaces.description: Namespace templates whose versions are incremented after matching mutation responses.

debounce.description: Window for batching repeated invalidations of the same rendered tag or namespace.

on_status.description: Upstream response statuses that trigger invalidation. Supports exact statuses like "200" and classes like "2xx".

TenantConfig.description: Multi-tenant routing. Selects a backend based on a tenant identifier from headers.

header.description: HTTP header used to identify the tenant.

backends.description: Maps tenant identifiers to target URLs.

default.description: Fallback target URL when no tenant header matches. If empty, unmatched tenants receive 404.

circuit_breaker.description: Name of a circuit breaker profile defined in top-level circuit_breakers.

targets.description: Additional upstream URLs for load balancing. Combined with target_url.

path_rewrite.description: Replaces the matched path before proxying. Supports regex back-references ($1, $2).

path_mode.description: How the upstream URL path is constructed. "replace": target_url path replaces incoming path. "append": incoming path appended to target_url path.

pool.description: Name of a backend_pools entry used for outbound connection isolation.

timeout.description: Maximum duration CSAR will wait for this upstream request. Streaming requests bypass this route timeout.

BackendPoolConfig.description: Outbound HTTP transport pool settings for a backend failure domain.

max_idle_conns.description: Maximum idle connections across all hosts in the pool.

max_idle_conns_per_host.description: Maximum idle connections retained per host.

max_conns_per_host.description: Maximum total connections per host. Zero means unlimited.

response_header_timeout.description: Maximum wait for upstream response headers. Zero disables the transport-level header timeout.

idle_conn_timeout.description: Maximum lifetime for idle keep-alive connections.

HealthCheckConfig.description: Active health checking for load-balanced targets.

timeout.description: Maximum time to wait for a health check response.

unhealthy_threshold.description: Consecutive failures before marking a target as unhealthy.

healthy_threshold.description: Consecutive successes before marking a target as healthy again.

BackendTLSRef.description: Outbound TLS configuration. Supports a policy name (string) or an inline object.

oneOf.description: Reference to a named backend_tls_policies entry.

BackendTLSConfig.description: Outbound TLS to an upstream. May reference a named backend_tls_policies entry via "use", with inline fields overriding policy values.

use.description: Reference to a backend_tls_policies entry. Inline fields override the policy's values.

ca_file.description: Path to a custom CA bundle for verifying the upstream cert.

cert_file.description: Client certificate (PEM) for mTLS to the upstream.

key_file.description: Client private key (PEM) for mTLS to the upstream.

BackendTLSPolicy.description: Terminal named outbound TLS configuration. Does not support "use" — policy definitions are plain value objects.

ca_file.description: Path to a custom CA bundle for verifying the upstream cert.

cert_file.description: Client certificate (PEM) for mTLS to the upstream.

key_file.description: Client private key (PEM) for mTLS to the upstream.

AuthzClientConfig.description: Connection settings for the csar-authz gRPC authorization service.

address.description: gRPC address of the csar-authz service.

ca_file.description: CA certificate for verifying the csar-authz server.

cert_file.description: Client certificate (PEM) for mTLS to csar-authz.

key_file.description: Client private key (PEM) for mTLS to csar-authz.

allow_insecure.description: Allow plaintext gRPC to csar-authz. Development only.

timeout.description: Per-call timeout for CheckAccess RPCs. Default: 500ms.

AuditClientConfig.description: Connection settings for the csar-audit gRPC ingest service.

address.description: gRPC address of the csar-audit service.

ca_file.description: CA certificate for verifying the csar-audit server.

cert_file.description: Client certificate (PEM) for mTLS to csar-audit.

key_file.description: Client private key (PEM) for mTLS to csar-audit.

allow_insecure.description: Allow plaintext gRPC to csar-audit. Development only.

timeout.description: Per-call timeout for RecordEvents RPCs. Default: 500ms.

oneOf.description: Reference to a named authz_policies entry.

AuthzRouteConfigObject.description: Per-route authorization via csar-authz. Strips spoofable headers, evaluates access policy, injects trusted headers.

use.description: Reference to a named authz_policies entry. Inline fields override policy values.

strip_headers.description: Headers unconditionally removed from client requests to prevent spoofing.

RouteConfig.description: Single route entry with x-csar-* extensions.

x-csar-headers.description: Static key-value pairs injected into every upstream request for this route.

max_response_size.description: Maximum response body size (bytes) for DLP/retry buffering. Prevents DoS. Default: 0 (unlimited).

x-csar-audit.description: Emit access audit events for this route. When omitted, POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE default to on (if audit client is configured); GET/HEAD/OPTIONS default to off.

PathConfig.description: Route definitions keyed by HTTP method (get, post, put, delete, patch, head, options).

ReadinessConfig.description: Readiness probe configuration with dependency health checks.

path.description: HTTP path for the readiness probe.

include_details.description: Include per-check details in the response body.

DebugHeadersConfig.description: Debug and traceability headers. Automatically disabled in prod unless explicitly enabled.

request_id_header.description: Header name for request ID propagation.

emit_wait_ms.description: Emit X-CSAR-Wait-MS on successful responses for this route.

transparent_retry.description: Enable transparent upstream 429 retry for this route.

emit_client_hint.description: Reserved for future use; not yet implemented. Whether the server should emit X-CSAR-Client-Limit guidance.
